nodes.label: Blog Post to LinkedIn Article Workflow
nodes.norm_label: blog post to linkedin article workflow

nodes.label: Canonical Hugo DOM Source of Truth
nodes.norm_label: canonical hugo dom source of truth

nodes.label: Agent Orchestration
nodes.rationale: The coordinator dispatches domain work to specialists so role boundaries, handoffs, and reviewer gates remain enforceable.

nodes.label: Shared State Drop-Box Pattern
nodes.rationale: Per-agent inbox entries and Scribe-owned merging remove shared-file conflicts so independent agents can run concurrently.

nodes.label: Reviewer Rejection Protocol
nodes.rationale: A rejected artifact must be revised independently by another agent, preventing the original author from bypassing reviewer judgment.

nodes.label: Squad Source of Truth Hierarchy
nodes.rationale: Static governance and roster files remain authoritative while histories and logs are derived append-only evidence.

nodes.label: Autonomous Completion
nodes.rationale: The agent should retain control until every requested item is implemented and verified.

nodes.label: Adversarial Solution Validation
nodes.rationale: Red-team, stress, integration, and user-experience checks are used to expose failure modes before completion.

nodes.label: CommonMark Portability
nodes.rationale: Simple CommonMark constructs keep documentation readable in raw form, git diffs, terminals, and standard renderers.

nodes.label: Modular Azure Terraform Architecture
nodes.rationale: Resource-type modules promote reuse, consistent configuration, and clear separation of infrastructure concerns.

nodes.label: Managed Identity Authentication
nodes.rationale: Managed identities avoid stored credentials and support least-privilege Azure access.

nodes.label: App Configuration and Key Vault Pattern
nodes.norm_label: app configuration and key vault pattern

nodes.label: Terraform Idempotency
nodes.rationale: Repeated applies should converge to zero changes, avoiding scripts and resources that recreate or conflict on every run.

nodes.label: Atomic Commits
nodes.rationale: One logical change per commit keeps history readable and supports automated changelog and CI workflows.

nodes.label: 95 Percent Confidence Gate
nodes.rationale: Clarification continues until requirements are sufficiently certain to avoid implementing an underspecified request.

nodes.label: Placeholder Self-Check
nodes.rationale: Generated artifacts must run without another developer filling in dummy values or unfinished blocks.

nodes.label: Canonical Hugo DOM Source of Truth
nodes.rationale: The deployed article DOM preserves the real rendered order, captions, links, and Hugo conversions more reliably than Markdown, RSS descriptions, or filenames.
nodes.norm_label: canonical hugo dom source of truth

nodes.label: Marker-Based Image Placement
nodes.rationale: Stable media markers preserve exact placement for opening images, adjacent figures, repeated prose, and flattened galleries where nearby-text anchoring is unreliable.

nodes.label: Explicit Publish Confirmation
nodes.rationale: Draft preparation is reversible but publishing is irreversible, so approval must be explicit and current.

nodes.label: Microsoft API Validation Workflow
nodes.rationale: Live official documentation and samples prevent hallucinated methods, wrong signatures, deprecated SDK patterns, and incorrect package names.

nodes.label: OIDC to UAMI to RBAC to Foundry Chain
nodes.norm_label: oidc to uami to rbac to foundry chain

nodes.label: Foundry Token Audience Fallback
nodes.rationale: The check retries with the Foundry audience after a Cognitive Services audience returns 401 because resource token requirements differ.

nodes.label: Single-Step Bearer Token Boundary
nodes.rationale: Token acquisition, inference, and assertion share one step so the bearer token never enters outputs, environment files, or logs.

nodes.label: Human-Reviewed Pull Request Gate
nodes.rationale: AI-generated text and images remain drafts until a person verifies technical rigor, voice, links, attribution, disclosure, and image rights.

nodes.label: Open PR Ledger Overlay
nodes.rationale: Ledger entries from open AI pull requests are overlaid before discovery so in-review topics participate in deduplication before reaching main.

nodes.label: Serialized Article Fan-Out
nodes.rationale: Candidate builds use max-parallel one to avoid races on branches and labels while fail-fast remains disabled so one bad candidate does not cancel the rest.

nodes.label: CODEOWNER Review Gate
nodes.rationale: Only an approved review by an identity listed in CODEOWNERS can promote an AI draft.

nodes.label: Approved PR Head Guard
nodes.rationale: Publication aborts if the PR head advances after approval, ensuring metadata updates apply only to the reviewed commit.

nodes.label: GitHub Issue Action Items
nodes.rationale: Retrospective actions become labeled GitHub issues because production data showed no completion for Markdown checklists and full completion after switching to issues.

nodes.label: Custom In-Repo Hugo Rebuild
nodes.rationale: A custom in-repo Hugo site preserves existing Markdown posts, post URLs, image paths, all images, and the current logo without adopting a third-party theme.

nodes.label: AI Review-Gated Publishing
nodes.rationale: AI-generated posts, images, and metadata must enter through pull requests for human review and must never publish directly to the main branch.

nodes.label: Accessible Motion Modernization
nodes.rationale: The visual refresh uses progressive parallax and scroll reveal while preserving the logo and respecting prefers-reduced-motion.

nodes.label: Hugo Migration Validation Gate
nodes.rationale: Production cutover remains gated on migration checks, Hugo builds, and staging or preview smoke tests even after all 36 Jekyll posts are represented under content/posts.

nodes.label: Default-On Vanilla Figure Lightbox
nodes.rationale: A lightweight vanilla-JavaScript lightbox avoids the unused jQuery dependency required by Lity, defaults on with a per-post opt-out, and includes keyboard and focus accessibility.

nodes.label: AI Article Pipeline MVP
nodes.rationale: The initial pipeline runs manually in GitHub Actions, generates reviewable drafts, and defers scheduling and hosted-agent promotion until the supervised phase.

nodes.label: Direct MVP Execution Topology
nodes.rationale: Direct model calls keep the MVP simple while prompts stabilize; promotion to Foundry hosted agents is deferred.

nodes.label: Least-Privilege GitHub App
nodes.rationale: The automation identity cannot self-approve pull requests, and branch protection requires Rodrigo's human approval.

nodes.label: Two-Lock Publishing Model
nodes.rationale: Rendering requires an approved pull-request merge plus draft:false and ai.reviewed_by; merging alone does not publish.

nodes.label: Per-Topic Lifecycle Ledger
nodes.rationale: One YAML file per topic records lifecycle state, while exact keys and semantic embeddings enforce a 0.82 novelty threshold against a rebuildable cache and published content index.

nodes.label: Agentic Tavily Discovery Pipeline
nodes.rationale: A ReAct tool-calling loop lets the model propose searches while the orchestrator executes Tavily, keeping its credential only in GitHub Secrets and requiring no new Azure infrastructure or Python dependencies.

nodes.label: Weekly Fail-Closed Article Fan-Out
nodes.rationale: A Monday 06:00 UTC run ranks all passing candidates, generates at most the configured top three, opens one draft pull request per article, and emits no pull request when any safety or budget gate fails.

nodes.label: Grounded Article Image Policy
nodes.rationale: Articles may use generated body images and allowlisted source images only with visible attribution, server-side URL validation, and an enforced RAI and copyright review checklist.

nodes.label: Tavily Key Rotation Run Gate
nodes.rationale: The previously pasted Tavily key is treated as compromised; rotation and storage only as TAVILY_API_KEY are blocking prerequisites for the first real weekly run.

nodes.label: English Processing and Spanish Publishing
nodes.rationale: All AI reasoning and working metadata use English in a single pass, while every reader-facing field remains Spanish from Spain and image prompts remain English.

nodes.label: Eager and Anticipatory Agent Routing
nodes.rationale: Useful agents start eagerly, downstream testing is anticipated, substantial work always starts a background Scribe, and quick facts stay with the coordinator.

nodes.label: Seven Prompt Safety Invariants
nodes.rationale: The audit verifies that translating processing prompts to English did not remove prompt-injection defenses, source validation, or secret-handling controls.

nodes.label: Lean Post-Work Turn
nodes.rationale: Keeping the coordinator's post-work turn compact reduces context-overflow risk after multi-agent fan-out.

nodes.label: Retrospective with Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Retrospective action items become GitHub Issues because production observations showed completion improving from 0% with Markdown checklists to 100% with issues.

nodes.label: SQL Tool Caveat
nodes.rationale: Cross-platform workflows must use filesystem state because the SQL tool is available only in CLI mode.

nodes.label: MCP Authentication Isolation
nodes.rationale: MCP servers often require service-specific credentials separate from existing CLI authentication, making authentication setup a documentation concern and a real integration blocker.

nodes.label: Cost First Unless Code
nodes.rationale: Cheap models handle non-code and mechanical work, while code and executable prompt design use standard-tier models where accuracy matters more.

nodes.label: Two-Layer State Model
nodes.rationale: Commit-scoped git notes keep agent context out of PR diffs, while the permanent state backend retains long-lived decisions and archives.

nodes.label: Per-Agent Note Namespaces
nodes.rationale: Separate namespaces prevent most concurrent note conflicts; shared research and review namespaces use append semantics.

nodes.label: Alignment Before Implementation
nodes.rationale: Proposals create alignment before code is written because changing a document is cheaper than refactoring implementation.

nodes.label: Dynamic Command Loading
nodes.rationale: Command modules are dynamically imported to keep CLI startup fast.

nodes.label: Filesystem State Portability
nodes.rationale: Filesystem-based .squad state is required for workflows that must work beyond the CLI-only SQL tool.

nodes.label: Minimal Context Sharing
nodes.rationale: Delegations share only required capabilities, decisions, and handoff context to avoid leaking internal state and secrets.

nodes.label: Filesystem Is the Mesh
nodes.rationale: Agents retain a local file interface while git or HTTP only materializes remote state onto disk.

nodes.label: Phased Mesh Rollout
nodes.rationale: The mesh begins as a convention and advances only when project-level decisions justify added complexity.

nodes.label: Progressive Verdicting
nodes.rationale: Each scenario verdict is posted immediately so model connection loss cannot erase all accumulated results.

nodes.label: Explicit Preference Precedence
nodes.rationale: Economy mode changes only automatic Layer 3 selection because persistent config, session directives, and charter preferences express user intent.

nodes.label: Draft-Only Mode
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 requires a mandatory human review gate and prohibits autonomous posting.

nodes.label: Per-Operation Token Use
nodes.rationale: Reading a named account token without switching global state isolates one operation and avoids breaking parallel agents.

nodes.label: Parallel Worktrees
nodes.rationale: One worktree per simultaneous issue prevents filesystem collisions and branch-switching overhead.

nodes.label: Final Outcome Recording
nodes.rationale: Recording confirmed outcomes rather than intermediate requests prevents stale history from poisoning later decisions.

nodes.label: Roster Confirmation Gate
nodes.rationale: No team files are created until the user explicitly confirms the proposed roster.

nodes.label: Members Header Contract
nodes.rationale: The exact Members heading is required because GitHub workflows use it for label automation.

nodes.label: Union Merge Driver for Squad State
nodes.norm_label: union merge driver for squad state

nodes.label: Research Then Execute
nodes.rationale: Research and analysis must end with a concrete action or explicit coordinator sign-off to be considered complete.

nodes.label: Persistent Model Preferences
nodes.rationale: Persistent Layer 0 configuration wins because it records the user's explicit quality and cost choice.

nodes.label: Topic-Based Notification Routing
nodes.rationale: Prevent alert fatigue and missed alerts caused by flooding every notification type into one channel.

nodes.label: Provider-Agnostic Notification Adapter
nodes.rationale: Keep routing configuration and message tags stable while swapping only the delivery-platform adapter.

nodes.label: Ambient Personal-Agent Discovery
nodes.rationale: Make user-level agents available across projects without requiring project-local duplication.

nodes.label: Ghost Protocol
nodes.rationale: Personal agents may advise from project state, but project agents retain write authority and conflict precedence.

nodes.label: PR Review Response Workflow
nodes.rationale: A code fix alone is insufficient because reviewers need a traceable explanation on each original review thread.

nodes.label: Individual Review Thread Reply
nodes.rationale: Replying separately lets reviewers verify each requested change without rereading the entire diff.

nodes.label: Review Thread Resolution
nodes.rationale: Agent-authored threads may be resolved after a complete fix, while human reviewer threads remain open for human confirmation.

nodes.label: Visual PR Review
nodes.rationale: Embed screenshots in the PR description so reviewers can inspect visual changes without checking out the branch.

nodes.label: Temporary Screenshot Orphan Branch
nodes.rationale: GitHub PR descriptions need image URLs, while feature branches should not retain generated screenshot artifacts.

nodes.label: Ralph Two-Pass Issue Scanning
nodes.rationale: Reduce GitHub API calls by filtering a lightweight issue list before hydrating full issue details.

nodes.label: Selective Issue Hydration
nodes.rationale: Fetch comments, body, and complete metadata only for issues that survive the first-pass ownership and status filters.

nodes.label: Reflect Learning Capture
nodes.rationale: Capture corrections, successes, and edge cases during work so the squad does not repeat mistakes.

nodes.label: Learning Promotion
nodes.rationale: Promote high-confidence team patterns to decisions and agent-specific patterns to append-only histories after approval.

nodes.label: Squad Release Process
nodes.rationale: Codify incident-tested checks and ownership boundaries that prevent broken npm publishes.

nodes.label: Pre-Publish Dependency Validation
nodes.rationale: Stop releases containing file, link, absolute-path, or non-semver dependency values before tagging.

nodes.label: Package-Directory npm Publish
nodes.rationale: Publishing after changing into each package directory avoids npm workspace hangs under two-factor authentication.

nodes.label: GITHUB_TOKEN Downstream Workflow Limitation
nodes.rationale: A release created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot trigger the downstream publish workflow, requiring manual dispatch or a different token.

nodes.label: Team Reskilling
nodes.rationale: Reduce per-agent context consumption by extracting repeated charter and history knowledge into reusable skills.

nodes.label: Shared Skill Extraction
nodes.rationale: Patterns repeated across three or more charters should become shared skills rather than remain duplicated.

nodes.label: Retrospective Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Retrospectives must happen on schedule and their action items must use a trackable system with ownership and notifications.

nodes.label: GitHub Issue Retro Action Items
nodes.rationale: GitHub Issues provide assignees, notifications, close events, and queryability that markdown checklists lack.

nodes.label: Coordinator Retro Blocking
nodes.rationale: An overdue retrospective blocks queue construction until a facilitator writes the log and creates issue-backed actions.

nodes.label: Strict Author Lockout
nodes.rationale: Prevent defensive self-revision loops by requiring an independently produced next version after rejection.

nodes.label: Reviewer Lockout Deadlock Escalation
nodes.rationale: Escalate to the user when every eligible revision author has been locked out rather than re-admitting a rejected author.

nodes.label: Secret Handling Policy
nodes.rationale: Prevent live credentials read by agents from being copied into automatically committed squad state.

nodes.label: Prohibited Environment File Reads
nodes.rationale: Use example or template environment files to understand schema without exposing live credentials.

nodes.label: Scribe Pre-Commit Secret Scan
nodes.rationale: Scan staged squad state and block the commit before a credential reaches git history.

nodes.label: Copilot Session Recovery
nodes.rationale: Recover interrupted agent work using persisted session history instead of guessing from an incomplete worktree.

nodes.label: Automated Session Filter
nodes.rationale: Exclude keep-alive and heartbeat sessions so high-volume automation does not obscure human-initiated work.

nodes.label: Checkpoint Recovery Context
nodes.rationale: Inspect turns, checkpoints, touched files, and linked references before deciding how to resume.

nodes.label: Zero Runtime Dependencies
nodes.rationale: Squad must remain a zero-dependency package implemented with Node.js built-ins.

nodes.label: fatal Error Pattern
nodes.rationale: User-facing failures should be concise and controlled rather than expose unhandled stack traces.

nodes.label: Squad Init Idempotency
nodes.rationale: Initialization skips existing user state, while upgrades overwrite only Squad-owned files.

nodes.label: Test Discipline
nodes.rationale: Keep tests synchronized with public API and filesystem changes so stale assertions do not block other contributors.

nodes.label: Same-Commit API Test Updates
nodes.rationale: Any public interface change must update its corresponding tests in the same commit.

nodes.label: Assertion-to-Disk Synchronization
nodes.rationale: Expected file counts and resource arrays must reflect actual files on disk.

nodes.label: Tiered Agent Memory
nodes.rationale: Load only task-relevant memory to reduce repeated context payloads while retaining durable knowledge on demand.

nodes.label: Scribe Memory Promotion
nodes.rationale: Scribe compresses session memory into cross-session summaries and later promotes stable facts into durable wiki knowledge.

nodes.label: Squad Versioning Policy
nodes.rationale: Prevent package mismatches and prerelease leaks that silently break npm workspace resolution.

nodes.label: Ephemeral Build Prerelease Versions
nodes.rationale: Build-suffixed versions exist only for local testing and must never be committed to dev or main.

nodes.label: SDK CLI and Root Version Synchronization
nodes.rationale: All three package versions must move in lockstep to preserve workspace resolution.
nodes.norm_label: sdk cli and root version synchronization

nodes.label: Windows Compatibility
nodes.rationale: Eliminate cross-platform failures caused by invalid timestamps, fragile git invocation, and path assumptions.

nodes.label: Temporary-File Git Commit Message
nodes.rationale: Use git commit with a message file because embedded newlines in PowerShell command arguments fail silently.

nodes.label: Platform-Aware Path Comparison
nodes.rationale: Compare paths case-insensitively on Windows and macOS but preserve Linux case sensitivity.

nodes.label: Squad Docs Build and Deploy Workflow
nodes.norm_label: squad docs build and deploy workflow

nodes.label: Team Roster Fallback
nodes.rationale: The workflow reads .squad/team.md first and falls back to .ai-team/team.md so both roster layouts can drive assignments.

nodes.label: Forbidden Path Stripping
nodes.rationale: Squad state and proposal paths are removed from the preview merge so internal coordination files are not shipped in a release.

nodes.label: Idempotent Tag Creation
nodes.rationale: The workflow checks for an existing version tag and skips tag and release creation when it already exists.

nodes.label: Lead Fallback Routing
nodes.rationale: Issues without a capability or domain match are assigned to the Lead for further analysis.

nodes.label: Live Published Post Index
nodes.rationale: Published state is derived from content/posts on every run so repository reality wins over stale ledger data and the system self-repairs after renames or deletions.

nodes.label: Underscore Template Convention
nodes.rationale: Topic files beginning with an underscore are treated as examples and ignored by the scanner.

nodes.label: Server-Side Domain Allowlist
nodes.rationale: The allowlist is controlled by the orchestrator rather than model output to constrain discovery and image retrieval to approved domains.

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Publication Date Validation
nodes.rationale: Only Tavily publication dates or structural URL dates are trusted; undated primary material cannot qualify a candidate as fresh.

nodes.label: Untrusted Source Content Handling
nodes.rationale: External content is sanitized and delimited as untrusted data so embedded instructions cannot control prompts, filesystem writes, or citations.

nodes.label: Source Image Download Security
nodes.rationale: Source images are constrained by allowlisted hosts, redirect revalidation, exact candidate URLs, magic-number checks, and a byte limit to mitigate SSRF and invalid downloads.

nodes.label: Safe YAML Serialization
nodes.rationale: yaml.safe_dump serializes untrusted model output without concatenating it into YAML and preserves escaping and Unicode.

nodes.label: Tool Mastery Before Upgrades
nodes.rationale: Better tools do not guarantee better work; practitioners should exhaust existing capabilities through skill before requesting replacements.

nodes.label: Frozen Requirements
nodes.rationale: Application development is easy only when functional requirements remain frozen, paralleling walking over frozen water.

nodes.label: SYS Schema Isolation
nodes.rationale: A narrow proxy package exposes file-listing results while preventing application schemas from receiving general access to SYS internals.

nodes.label: SearchFiles Memory Risk
nodes.rationale: Recursive searches with many matching files can consume substantial memory because X$KRBMSFT is an in-memory table.

nodes.label: Requirements Control
nodes.rationale: System requirements are compared to balloons that remain controlled only while held firmly.

nodes.label: Publish-Time Minification
nodes.rationale: Minification runs on packaged Release output so downloaded assets shrink without changing the original development source files.

nodes.label: UsingTask Over Import
nodes.rationale: UsingTask points to source-controlled DLLs so every contributor uses the same Ajax Minifier version and location.

nodes.label: Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP)
nodes.rationale: Modularizes cross-cutting functionality to reduce coupling and mitigate limitations that emerge in object-oriented systems.

nodes.label: Separation of Concerns
nodes.rationale: Separates common application-wide functionality from functionality specific to each module.

nodes.label: Minimal Extensible AOP Framework
nodes.rationale: A custom framework was preferred to large complex products to provide fine control, comfort, extensibility, and technology-independent aspect implementations.

nodes.label: Aspect Priority Ordering
nodes.rationale: Explicit priorities decouple aspect execution order from source attribute order and preserve architectural governance as aspects are added or removed.

nodes.label: Logging as an Aspect
nodes.rationale: An attribute hides standardized event messages, prevents accidental omission, and strengthens logging governance.

nodes.label: Microsoft Enterprise Library Logging Application Block
nodes.norm_label: microsoft enterprise library logging application block

nodes.label: Exception Handling as an Aspect
nodes.rationale: Interception centralizes exception policies, improves governance, and prevents sensitive stack traces from reaching users.

nodes.label: Layered Exception Strategy
nodes.rationale: Propagate within a layer, wrap across layer boundaries, and replace at the UI boundary to avoid exposing sensitive information.

nodes.label: Dynamic Entity Configuration Loading
nodes.rationale: Reflection plus dynamic dispatch loads configuration classes automatically to reduce cyclomatic complexity and coupling in large models.

nodes.label: Embedded Resource Dataset
nodes.rationale: Stores large seed datasets outside source-code object initializers to avoid Visual Studio degradation.

nodes.label: Reflection over Entity Framework Internal APIs
nodes.rationale: Internal metadata is required for mappings unavailable through the public Entity Framework 5 API, at the cost of version fragility.
nodes.norm_label: reflection over entity framework internal apis

nodes.label: Degenerated Agile Build-and-Deploy Delivery
nodes.rationale: Unhealthy nonstop delivery pressure favors speed-focused developer behavior at the expense of polish and knowledge transfer.

nodes.label: Balanced Ninja-Zen Programmer
nodes.rationale: Organizations need both rapid problem solving and patient quality-oriented architecture, ideally combined in one balanced practitioner.

nodes.label: Reciprocal Importance of Others' Work
nodes.rationale: Treating other people's work as important creates a reciprocal culture in which they value yours.

nodes.label: Technical Code Audit
nodes.rationale: Combining compiled code metrics with physical line, blank, and documentation counts gives a fuller maintainability picture than LOC alone.

nodes.label: Developer-Centered Platform Design
nodes.rationale: .NET, C#, and Visual Studio were designed to feel natural to programmers and remove operational friction so they could focus on application quality.

nodes.label: Synchronized APIM and Stripe Product IDs
nodes.rationale: Matching product identifiers across APIM and Stripe connects measured consumption to the correct billable product.
nodes.norm_label: synchronized apim and stripe product ids

nodes.label: Delegated Subscription Management
nodes.rationale: An external web application handles billing data and subscription workflows that the built-in Developer Portal cannot customize.

nodes.label: Cosmos DB Change Feed and Transactional Batches
nodes.norm_label: cosmos db change feed and transactional batches

nodes.label: Cloud Native Semantic Kernel article in CompartiMOSS 56
nodes.norm_label: cloud native semantic kernel article in compartimoss 56

nodes.label: Cloud Native AI Architecture
nodes.rationale: The article combines Semantic Kernel and Qdrant to build a fully cloud-native artificial-intelligence architecture.

nodes.label: Integrating Other LLMs with Semantic Kernel
nodes.norm_label: integrating other llms with semantic kernel

nodes.label: String-encoded JSON Function Contract
nodes.rationale: Semantic Kernel functions exchange strings, so the native function returns the model response as JSON text that the caller later deserializes into a typed response.

nodes.label: Scoped Semantic Kernel Lifetime
nodes.rationale: Web applications should register the kernel as scoped because it manages state and contexts that could cause side effects when shared as a singleton.

nodes.label: Factory for Complex Plugin Initialization
nodes.rationale: A factory is recommended when native-function initialization is complex or resource-intensive.

nodes.label: Answer Score Threshold
nodes.rationale: The demo accepts answers at or above a 0.7 confidence score and returns HTTP 404 when the model cannot produce a sufficiently reliable answer.

nodes.label: Corporate ChatGPT
nodes.rationale: The proposed low-code solution gives organizational users ChatGPT-like capabilities while retaining control over consumption, access, and exposure of private data.

nodes.label: Enterprise AI Data and Access Control
nodes.rationale: Keeping AI inside controlled organizational tools mitigates security risks caused by users sending private, sensitive, or confidential information to public AI services.
nodes.norm_label: enterprise ai data and access control

nodes.label: Planning for Success with Semantic Kernel Planners
nodes.norm_label: planning for success with semantic kernel planners

nodes.label: Semantic Kernel Planners
nodes.rationale: Planners use AI to select and combine registered plugin functions into steps that satisfy a user goal when developers cannot enumerate every possible workflow.

nodes.label: Action Planner
nodes.rationale: Action Planner chooses one best-aligned function, making it suitable for intent routing and low-latency scenarios.

nodes.label: Sequential Planner
nodes.rationale: Sequential Planner creates serial multi-function plans and passes each function output into the next function.

nodes.label: Stepwise Planner
nodes.rationale: Stepwise Planner dynamically explores functions and can revise its approach while solving interconnected multi-step requests.

nodes.label: Planner Relevancy Threshold
nodes.rationale: Filtering functions below a relevance threshold produces more focused and efficient plans and raises the chance of successfully satisfying complex goals.

nodes.label: Function Descriptions as Planner Metadata
nodes.rationale: Planners identify relevant functions and parameters from their descriptions, so descriptions should be verbose, concrete, complete, and unambiguous.

nodes.label: Planner Performance, Cost, and Reliability Tradeoffs
nodes.rationale: Planners are slow, make multiple costly LLM calls, and can generate defective plans because generation is stochastic; exclusions, retries, error handling, and precise descriptions mitigate these risks.
nodes.norm_label: planner performance, cost, and reliability tradeoffs

nodes.label: Agent Harness
nodes.rationale: Agent Harness forms the operational boundary where file access, tool approvals, and memory types make a capable agent governable; the model reasons, but the harness controls execution.

nodes.label: Tool Approval Strategies
nodes.rationale: Explicit auto-approved, conditional, and mandatory-human approval levels prevent sensitive or irreversible actions from executing solely on model initiative.

nodes.label: Layered Agent Memory
nodes.rationale: Separating session, operational, preference, and externally retrieved memory avoids excessive, incorrect, or unsafe retention.

nodes.label: Agent Observability
nodes.rationale: Production operation requires reconstructing user input, context retrieval, tool selection, approvals, final output, cost, latency, and failures across the complete agent flow.

nodes.label: Multi-agent Specialization
nodes.rationale: Multiple agents are justified only when each has a clear one-sentence responsibility and contract; overlapping responsibilities rapidly make the system opaque.

nodes.label: Durable Agent Workflows
nodes.rationale: Long-running processes with human waits, external calls, retries, compensations, and notifications should be modeled as durable workflows rather than one long model conversation.

nodes.label: Build and Run Agents at Scale with Microsoft Foundry
nodes.norm_label: build and run agents at scale with microsoft foundry

nodes.label: Microsoft Agent Framework at BUILD 2026 Announcement
nodes.norm_label: microsoft agent framework at build 2026 announcement

nodes.label: Agent Harness: Working with Your Data Safely
nodes.norm_label: agent harness: working with your data safely

nodes.label: Strict Origin When Cross Origin Policy
nodes.norm_label: strict origin when cross origin policy

nodes.label: Selective Red Color
nodes.rationale: Red is isolated against the grayscale figures and wall to make the balloons the visual focus.

nodes.label: Hands Compressing a Rusted Coil Spring
nodes.norm_label: hands compressing a rusted coil spring

nodes.label: Abstract Loop Glyph Composition
nodes.rationale: The thumbnail uses a consistent interwoven-loop motif with varied colors and shapes to create visual diversity while preserving a unified identity.

nodes.label: Estimation as Blind Guesswork
nodes.rationale: The blindfolded shooter and toy dart gun satirize project effort estimation as an arbitrary selection among predefined time ranges.

nodes.label: Debugger Process Attachment
nodes.rationale: Attaching to a process can potentially harm the computer; the user should not attach when the displayed information is suspicious or uncertain.

nodes.label: IIS Application Pool Process Model Identity
nodes.norm_label: iis application pool process model identity

nodes.label: Developer Isolation
nodes.rationale: The comic contrasts finding another developer with the same error against the helpless isolation caused by an old thread that has no answer.

nodes.label: Minimum Answer Score Threshold
nodes.rationale: Only return HTTP 200 when the deserialized answer score exceeds 0.7; otherwise return HTTP 404 with the observed score.
